input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover the current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target builds need the Windows SDK and WinUI tooling. The easiest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, for a minimal install via command line:
# (adjust --add lines to taste)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Component.Windows11SDK.22621 `
              --quiet --wait"
```

## 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

### Option A — Use the MAUI workload's built-in provisioning

```powershell
# Create a throwaway project and build for Android;
# the first build auto-downloads the Android SDK & NDK
dotnet new maui -n MauiProbe
cd MauiProbe
dotnet build -f net9.0-android   # triggers SDK/NDK download
cd .. && Remove-Item -Recurse MauiProbe
```

### Option B — Manual install via Android command-line tools

```powershell
# 1. Download command-line tools
$cmdLineUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest $cmdLineUrl -OutFile cmdline-tools.zip
Expand-Archive cmdline-tools.zip -DestinationPath "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools"
Rename-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\cmdline-tools" "latest"

# 2. Add to PATH for this session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
$env:PATH += ";$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin;$env:ANDROID_HOME\platform-tools"

# 3. Discover available platform & build-tools versions dynamically
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"      | Select-Object -First 10

# 4. Install what MAUI needs (adjust versions from output above)
sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# 5. Accept all licenses
sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to each
```

### Persist ANDROID_HOME

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk",
  "User"
)
```

## 5 — (Optional) Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
avdmanager create avd -n Pixel7 -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_7"
emulator -avd Pixel7
```

## 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying unpackaged WinUI 3 / MAUI apps:

```powershell
# Requires an elevated (admin) PowerShell
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7 — Verify Everything

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui && cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8 — Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What                    | Command                                                      |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| Latest .NET SDK         | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK`                         |
| Available MAUI workload | `dotnet workload search maui`                                |
| Android platforms       | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "platforms;android-"`    |
| Android build-tools     | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "build-tools;"`          |
| Windows SDK versions    | `winget search "Windows SDK"`                                |
| Installed workloads     | `dotnet workload list`                                       |
| MAUI TFM versions       | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` then inspect the `.csproj`       |

---

**Key gotcha:** If you're on .NET 10 preview, replace `net9.0-*` TFMs with `net10.0-*`. The `dotnet workload search` and `sdkmanager --list` commands ensure you always pull the right versions rather than hard-coding them.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. This is a knowledge/guidance request — I don't need to run commands or explore a codebase. I'll provide a comprehensive plan directly.
